member.name: Alice Macdonald
member.synopsis: Alice Macdonald is the Labour (Co-op) MP for Norwich North , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Fertility: Health Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what his Department's planned timetable is for completing its assessment of the provision of NHS-commissioned fertility services currently in place across Integrated Care Boards.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises that access to National Health Service funded fertility services is variable in England and is looking into achievable ambitions to improve access to fertility services. This includes understanding the offer made by fertility ser...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Airports: Fees and Charges
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, whether her Department is taking steps to regulate airport drop off charges.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department has no plans to regulate car parking and drop-off charges at UK airports. Decisions on the provision and pricing of airport parking remain a commercial matter for individual operators. Parking revenue, including that from drop-off charges, ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: IVF
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, when his Department plans to complete the assessment of NHS commissioned fertility services announced in the Women’s health strategy.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government published the Women's Health Strategy on 15 April, which commits to ensuring that every woman can easily access fertility services, and we are currently working to assess the current provision of National Health Service commissioned fertili...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Airports: Fees and Charges
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, whether she has had recent discussions with stakeholders on steps to regulate airport drop off charges.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department regularly engages with airports and other stakeholders on a wide range of matters, such as surface access arrangements which includes parking and drop-off charges. Airports in the UK are managed and operated as private businesses. While th...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Employment Schemes: Young People
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what steps his Department is taking to support young people with SEND into work.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is taking action to support disabled young people and those with long-term health conditions into work, including young people with Special Educational Needs (SEND). Pathways to Work and the Youth Guarantee will provide disabled young peopl...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Work and Pensions

writtenQuestions.heading: Food: Labelling
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment he has made of the potential impact of (a) new product labelling standards, including the proportion by weight of dominant food compounds such as salt and sugar and (b) developing n
writtenQuestions.answerText: Food labelling legislation requires that pre-packaged food and drinks include a nutrition declaration, which provides information on key nutrients, including salt and sugar. In addition, voluntary front-of-pack nutrition labelling, most commonly the multi...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Arts: Norwich
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what steps her Department is taking to support creative industries in Norwich.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Creative industries clusters across the UK, including in Norwich, are crucial to the delivery of our Growth Mission. Our Creative Industries Sector Plan published last year includes a strong universal offer to drive growth in the creative industries in ev...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: UNESCO: Literature
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what steps her Department is taking to (a) support and (b) promote UNESCO Cities of Literature in the UK.
writtenQuestions.answerText: DCMS supports the UK’s UNESCO designations through its relationship with the UK National Commission for UNESCO (UKNC), which collaborates closely with the UK UNESCO Creative Cities across the UK, including Cities of Literature.UKNC promotes the UK’s UNESC...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Motor Vehicles: Brakes
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what assessment she has made on the potential impact of increasing the frequency of Antilock Breaking Systems (ABS) for large vehicles, including large estate cars and SUVs, on road safety; and what assessment
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department has not made any assessment of the impact of increasing the frequency at which Anti-Lock Braking Systems (ABS) operate or start to operate as this does not reflect how these systems are regulated in practice. The requirements for ABS have b...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.answerText: Parking operators are incentivised to become members of one of the two accredited trade associations, the BPA and IPC, as without such membership operators cannot access DVLA data, meaning they cannot pursue charges by post or provide access to an indepen...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Parking: Fines
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what action has been taken to ensure the appeal process is effective for private parking companies that do not subscribe to the British Parking Association or International Parki
writtenQuestions.answerText: Parking operators are incentivised to become members of one of the two accredited trade associations, the BPA and IPC, as without such membership operators cannot access DVLA data, meaning they cannot pursue charges by post or provide access to an indepen...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Domestic Abuse: Technology
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what information her Department holds on the number of technology-facilitated abuse incidents reported to the police in each of the last five years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Home Office does not hold data on technology-facilitated abuse incidents recorded by police forces in England and Wales.The rapid development of technology has provided additional spaces for technology facilitated abuse and violence against women and ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Domestic Abuse: Technology
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, whether the Violence Against Women and Girls strategy and delivery plan will include (a) funding and (b) measures to help tackle technology-facilitated domestic abuse.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Tackling violence against women and girls (VAWG) in all of its forms, including abuse facilitated by technology, is a top priority for this government, with an unprecedented mission to halve it within a decade. The rapid development of technology has prov...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Conflict Resolution: Women
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, if she will consider adding Sudan as a focus country within the Women Peace and Security National Action Plan.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK is appalled at the severe impact of the conflict in Sudan on women and girls, including widespread sexual violence.Through our leadership roles at the UN Security Council and Human Rights Council, we have championed evidence collection and accounta...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Mefenamic Acid
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment his Department has made of the adequacy of the (a) supply and (b) availability of mefenamic acid for people in (i) the East of England and (ii) England.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department has a responsibility to work with United Kingdom medicine license holders to help ensure continuity of supply. We monitor and manage medicine supply issues at a national level so that stocks remain available to meet regional and local deman...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Floods: Fire and Rescue Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what assessment she has made of the potential merits of establishing a statutory duty for fire and rescue services in England to respond to flooding incidents.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Protecting communities from flooding is a key priority for this Government. Fire and Rescue Authorities already have duties under the Civil Contingencies Act 2004 to prepare for emergencies, including major flooding. However, we recognise the interest in ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Unemployment: Norfolk
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what estimate she has made of the number of people aged between 16 and 24 years old that are not in education, employment or training in (a) Norwich North constituency and (b) Norfolk.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department publishes statistics on 16-24-year-olds that are not in education, employment or training (NEET) for England in the Labour Force Survey (LFS). The most recent dataset is available here: https://explore-education-statistics.service.gov.uk/fi...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: Water Supply: Norfolk
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what recent assessment he has made of the pressures on water resources in Norfolk; and what steps he is taking with local partners to reduce those pressures.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The entire eastern England region is classified as seriously water stressed. Norfolk is one of the driest parts of the country with only 600mm rainfall per year which is two thirds of the national average. Norfolk supports some of England’s most precious ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Mental Health Services: Norfolk
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to improve adult mental health services in Norfolk.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Long waits for mental health services are being driven by increasing demand in a system in desperate need of change, including in NorfolkThe Government is piloting innovative models of care in the community, including six neighbourhood adult mental health...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Opioids: Death

writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care
